qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated REST API allows arbitrary configuration injection and full configuration disclosure in Traefik v1.7
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and vulnerable code: this finding covers unauthenticated REST/API endpoints in provider/rest/rest.go and api/handler.go, while the advisory covers fragmented TLS ClientHello handling in Traefik's SNI pre-sniff path causing fallback to a default non-mTLS TLS config.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described fragmented TLS ClientHello SNI pre-sniff failure or the resulting fallback to a weaker default TLS configuration that bypasses route-level mTLS.
evidence: The advisory describes router.go ServeTCP/clientHelloInfo behavior where fragmented TLS ClientHello records cause SNI extraction to fail with EOF and return an empty SNI.
The qualifying finding describes unauthenticated PUT /api/providers/rest and GET /api endpoints that read or mutate Traefik v1.7 dynamic configuration.
The qualifying finding's code locations and reproduction do not exercise ClientHello fragmentation, SNI parsing, TLS option selection, client certificate authentication, or route-level mTLS bypass.
